_README: Optional connection-type exceptions for Hub Diagnostics. Most integrations need NO entry here: the connection type is derived from the hub's own isNetwork flag (LAN => lan_direct, otherwise cloud) and the display name from the parent-app name. Add an entry ONLY when that derivation is wrong for one of your integrations -- e.g. a LAN bridge whose child devices report isNetwork=true (they would mis-derive to lan_direct instead of lan_bridge), or a locally-addressed integration the hub flags isNetwork=false (it would mis-derive to cloud instead of lan_direct). To use: upload this file to the hub's File Manager as hub_diagnostics_integration_overrides.json, then open the Hub Diagnostics app and Save the settings page to apply. Keys are lowercase substrings matched against a device's parent-app name. 'conn' is one of: paired, lan_direct, lan_bridge, cloud, virtual, hubmesh, other. 'name' overrides the display name (rarely needed -- the cleaned parent-app name is usually correct). Either field may be omitted. Any key starting with an underscore is ignored, so the _example entry below is inactive: copy it and remove the leading underscore from the key to enable it.
